name: Basic Risotto
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat butter and olive oil in a 14-inch by 11-inch (22 cm) dish or large casserole dish, uncovered.
Cook at high heat for 2 minutes.
2. Add onions and stir to coat. Cook, uncovered at high heat for an additional 4 minutes.
3. Add rice and stir to coat. Cook, uncovered for another 4 minutes.
4. Stir in broth (homemade is best; it has the best flavor). Cook, uncovered for 12 more minutes.
5. Remove from heat. Let stand uncovered for 5 minutes to let rice absorb remaining liquid, stirring several times.
6. Stir in salt, pepper, and Parmesan cheese if desired.
Note: Arborio rice is the best rice for Risotto. It is a short-grained Italian rice; available in grocery stores in cities with large Italian populations, Italian food shops, and gourmet food stores. Using North American rice will not produce the right creamy texture.
